libraryDescription:
The VCSSL GUI library is the standard library for handling graphical user interfaces in VCSSL.

Because the GUI library is relatively large, it is difficult to use it only by referring to the function specifications described here.For concrete usage examples, see the Official VCSSL GUI Development Guide.

- Note -
In programs using a GUI, event handlers are essential. In VCSSL, event handlers are executed in independent threads.The current version of RINEARN VCSSL Runtime does not yet provide sufficient support for multithreaded processing. This is because the current basic design of the VCSSL engine still partially follows the design of early VCSSL engines, which were developed assuming only single-threaded execution.As a result, depending on the processing performed, several issues including logical inconsistencies may occur. When writing event-handler processing in RINEARN VCSSL Runtime, do so with this situation in mind.At present, the following kinds of issues may occur.
- Inconsistent indexes when the same array variable is accessed simultaneously from multiple threads
In the current VCSSL engine, some modules that access array-variable values still retain designs intended only for single-threaded execution. Because of this, if the same array variable is accessed simultaneously, incorrect index values may be referenced.
- Emergency shutdown of the engine caused by extremely frequent simultaneous multithreaded access to the same function
In principle, the same function can be executed by multiple threads. In that case, arguments and local variables are secured separately for each thread so that they are not mixed up between threads. However, if simultaneous access is concentrated within too short an interval, this processing may fail to keep up, and the engine may perform an emergency shutdown to prevent inconsistencies. Overlap in function execution time is not the issue. The problem is when the moments of calling the function, that is, passing arguments, overlap.
Because issues such as the above may occur, when writing processing inside event handlers in the current RINEARN VCSSL Runtime, design it with the above points in mind and follow guidelines such as the following.
- In event handlers, keep processing lightweight, such as storing event information in global variables, and perform the actual processing in the main thread whenever possible

- Do not access array variables that are frequently accessed in the main thread from event handlers or other threads
However, if the main thread, that is, the thread executing the automatically started processing at launch, finishes after only short tasks such as constructing the GUI screen, and thereafter almost all processing occurs only from GUI event handlers in response to user operations, then the above points do not need to be considered very much.

variable113.name: INNER
variable113.description: The constant used to specify the inner area of a GUI component. It is used in the getComponentSize function.

variable114.name: OUTER
variable114.description: The constant used to specify the outer area of a GUI component. It is used in the getComponentSize function.

variable115.name: LISTED
variable115.description: The constant used to specify all listed items. It is used in the setComponentStringArray and getComponentStringArray functions.

variable116.name: SELECTED
variable116.description: The constant used to specify all selected items. It is used in the setComponentStringArray and getComponentStringArray functions.

function0.name: mountComponent
function0.declaration: void mountComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function0.description: Mounts a GUI component onto another GUI component.
function0.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be mounted. 
(int type) parentComponentID : The ID of the destination GUI component.

function1.name: demountComponent
function1.declaration: void demountComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function1.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be demounted. 
(int type) parentComponentID : The ID of the current parent GUI component.

function2.name: deleteComponent
function2.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be disposed.

function3.name: newWindow
function3.declaration: int newWindow( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )
function3.description: Creates a window, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function3.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) title : The window title.

function4.name: newButton
function4.declaration: int newButton( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function4.description: Creates a button, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function4.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed on the button.

function5.name: newTextField
function5.declaration: int newTextField( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function5.description: Creates a text field, which is a component for entering a single line of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function5.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed as the initial value.

function6.name: newTextArea
function6.declaration: int newTextArea( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function6.description: Creates a text area, which is a component for entering multiple lines of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function6.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed as the initial value.

function7.name: newTextLabel
function7.declaration: int newTextLabel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function7.description: Creates a text label, which is a component for displaying a single line of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function7.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text to be displayed.

function8.name: newImageLabel
function8.declaration: int newImageLabel( int x, int y, int w, int h, int graphicsID )
function8.description: Creates an image label, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function8.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function9.name: newCheckBox
function9.declaration: int newCheckBox( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text, boolean b )
function9.description: Creates a check box, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function9.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text to be displayed. 
(boolean type) b : The initial value.

function10.name: newSelectField
function10.declaration: int newSelectField( int x, int y, int w, int h, string str[  ] )
function10.description: Creates a select field, which is a single-line component allowing one item to be selected from choices, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).
function10.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string[] type) str : The choices.

function11.name: newSelectArea
function11.declaration: int newSelectArea( int x, int y, int w, int h, string str[  ] )
function11.description: Creates a select area, which is a multi-line component allowing multiple items to be selected from choices, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).
function11.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string[] type) str : The choices.

function12.name: newVerticalSlider
function12.declaration: int newVerticalSlider( int x, int y, int w, int h, float value )
function12.description: Creates a vertical slider for operating a float value in the range from 0.0 to 1.0, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).
function12.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(float type) value : The initial value.

function13.declaration: int newVerticalSlider( int x, int y, int w, int h, float value, float min, float max )

function13.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(float type) value : The initial value. 
(float type) min : The minimum value. 
(float type) max : The maximum value.

function18.declaration: int newPanel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )

width : The width. 
height : The height.

function20.declaration: int newFlowPanel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )

function21.name: newTabPanel
function21.declaration: int newTabPanel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )
function21.description: Creates a panel for tab display, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID). Use this panel by placing another panel on it.
function21.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) title : The panel title.

function22.name: newScrollPanel
function22.declaration: int newScrollPanel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )
function22.description: Creates a panel for scroll display, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID). Use this panel by placing another panel on it.
function22.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) title : The panel title.

function23.name: paintComponent
function23.description: Repaints a GUI component.Repainting is necessary when the displayed text or graphics resource has changed.It is also necessary when the displayed content of child components mounted on the component has changed.
function23.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function24.name: setComponentLocation
function24.declaration: void setComponentLocation( int componentID, int x, int y )
function24.description: Sets the location of a GUI component.
function24.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

function25.name: getComponentLocation
function25.description: Gets the location of a GUI component.
function25.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function25.returnValue: (int[] type) The top-left corner coordinates, where [0] is X and [1] is Y.

function26.name: setComponentSize
function26.declaration: void setComponentSize( int componentID, int width, int height )
function26.description: Sets the size of a GUI component.
function26.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function27.name: getComponentSize
function27.description: Gets the size of a GUI component.
function27.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function27.returnValue: (int[] type) The size, where [0] is the width and [1] is the height.

function28.name: getComponentSize
function28.declaration: int[ ] getComponentSize( int componentID, int option )
function28.description: Gets the size of a specific part of a GUI component.
function28.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. (int type) option : The part selection option (OUTER or INNER).
function28.returnValue: (int[] type) The size, where [0] is the width and [1] is the height.

function29.name: setComponentColor
function29.declaration: void setComponentColor( int componentID, int fr, int fg, int fb, int fa, int br, int bg, int bb, int ba )
function29.description: Sets the colors of a GUI component.
function29.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) fr : The red component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fg : The green component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fb : The blue component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fa : The alpha component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) br : The red component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) bg : The green component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) bb : The blue component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) ba : The alpha component of the background color (0 to 255).

function30.name: setComponentColor
function30.declaration: void setComponentColor( int componentID, int fgRGBA[ ], int bgRGBA[ ] )
function30.description: Sets the colors of a GUI component.
function30.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int[] type) fgRGBA : The array storing the foreground color components, where [0] is red, [1] is green, [2] is blue, and [3] is alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255. 
(int[] type) bgRGBA : The array storing the background color components, where [0] is red, [1] is green, [2] is blue, and [3] is alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function31.name: getComponentColor
function31.description: Returns the colors of a GUI component.
function31.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function31.returnValue: An int[][] array storing the color components, where [0][0] is the foreground red, [0][1] is the foreground green, [0][2] is the foreground blue, [0][3] is the foreground alpha, [1][0] is the background red, [1][1] is the background green, [1][2] is the background blue, and [1][3] is the background alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function32.name: setComponentText
function32.declaration: void setComponentText( int componentID, string text )
function32.description: Sets the text of a GUI component.
function32.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(string type) text : The text.

function33.name: getComponentText
function33.description: Gets the text of a GUI component.
function33.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function34.name: setComponentText
function34.declaration: void setComponentText( int componentID, string text[ ], int option )
function34.description: Sets the text data of a GUI component.
function34.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. (string[] type) text : The text data. (int type) option : The option (LISTED or SELECTED).

function35.name: getComponentText
function35.declaration: string[ ] getComponentText( int componentID, int option )
function35.description: Gets the text data of a GUI component.
function35.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. (int type) option : The option (LISTED or SELECTED).

function36.declaration: void setComponentImage( int componentID, int graphicsID )
function36.description: Sets the image of a GUI component.
function36.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function37.name: getComponentImage
function37.description: Returns the ID of the graphics resource storing the image of a GUI component.
function37.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function37.returnValue: (int type) The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function38.name: showComponent
function38.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function39.name: hideComponent
function39.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function40.parameters:

(string type) name : The font name, depending on the implementation and environment.

function41.name: setComponentFontSize
function41.declaration: void setComponentFontSize( int componentID, int size )
function41.description: Sets the font size of a GUI component.
function41.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function42.name: setComponentFontBold
function42.declaration: void setComponentFontBold( int componentID, bool isBold )
function42.description: Enables or disables bold style for the font of a GUI component. It cannot be used together with italic style.
function42.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(bool type) isBold : Whether to enable bold style.

function43.name: setComponentFontItalic
function43.declaration: void setComponentFontItalic( int componentID, bool isItalic )

function56.name: addComponent
function56.declaration: void addComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function56.description: Mounts a GUI component on another GUI component. Since VCSSL 3, the newer mountComponent function has been supported. This function is supported for compatibility.
function56.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to mount. 
(int type) componentID : The ID of the destination GUI component.

function57.name: removeComponent
function57.declaration: void removeComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function57.description: Demounts a GUI component. Since VCSSL 3, the newer demountComponent function has been supported. This function is supported for compatibility.
function57.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to demount. 
(int type) componentID : The ID of the current parent GUI component.

function58.name: newGraphicsLabel
function58.declaration: int newGraphicsLabel( int x, int y, int w, int h, int graphicsID )
function58.description: Creates a graphics label, which is a component displaying drawn content or an image, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).Since VCSSL 3.3.24, the newer newImageLabel function has been supported.This function is supported for compatibility.
function58.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID of the displayed content.

function59.name: setComponentGraphics

function59.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.

function60.name: getComponentGraphics
function60.description: Returns the graphics resource ID of a GUI component.Since VCSSL 3.3.24, the newer getComponentImage function has been supported.This function is supported for compatibility.
function60.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function0.name: mountComponent
function0.declaration: void mountComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function0.description: Mounts a GUI component onto another GUI component.
function0.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be mounted. 
(int type) parentComponentID : The ID of the destination GUI component.

function1.name: demountComponent
function1.declaration: void demountComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function1.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be demounted. 
(int type) parentComponentID : The ID of the current parent GUI component.

function2.name: deleteComponent
function2.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to be disposed.

function3.name: newWindow
function3.declaration: int newWindow( int x, int y, int w, int h, string title )
function3.description: Creates a window, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function3.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) title : The window title.

function4.name: newButton
function4.declaration: int newButton( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function4.description: Creates a button, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function4.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed on the button.

function5.name: newTextField
function5.declaration: int newTextField( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function5.description: Creates a text field, which is a component for entering a single line of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function5.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed as the initial value.

function6.name: newTextArea
function6.declaration: int newTextArea( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function6.description: Creates a text area, which is a component for entering multiple lines of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.
function6.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text displayed as the initial value.

function7.name: newTextLabel
function7.declaration: int newTextLabel( int x, int y, int w, int h, string text )
function7.description: Creates a text label, which is a component for displaying a single line of text, assigns a unique identifier (GUI component ID), and returns it.

(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string type) text : The text to be displayed.

function10.name: newSelectField
function10.declaration: int newSelectField( int x, int y, int w, int h, string str[  ] )
function10.description: Creates a select field, which is a single-line component allowing one item to be selected from choices, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).

(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(string[] type) str : The choices.

function11.name: newSelectArea
function11.declaration: int newSelectArea( int x, int y, int w, int h, string str[  ] )

function23.name: paintComponent
function23.description: Repaints a GUI component.Repainting is necessary when the displayed text or graphics resource has changed.It is also necessary when the displayed content of child components mounted on the component has changed.
function23.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function24.name: setComponentLocation
function24.declaration: void setComponentLocation( int componentID, int x, int y )
function24.description: Sets the location of a GUI component.
function24.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

function25.name: getComponentLocation
function25.description: Gets the location of a GUI component.
function25.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function25.returnValue: (int[] type) The top-left corner coordinates, where [0] is X and [1] is Y.

function26.name: setComponentSize
function26.declaration: void setComponentSize( int componentID, int width, int height )
function26.description: Sets the size of a GUI component.
function26.parameters:

(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function27.name: getComponentSize
function27.description: Gets the size of a GUI component.
function27.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function27.returnValue: (int[] type) The size, where [0] is the width and [1] is the height.

function28.name: getComponentSize
function28.declaration: int[ ] getComponentSize( int componentID, int option )
function28.description: Gets the size of a specific part of a GUI component.
function28.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. (int type) option : The part selection option (OUTER or INNER).
function28.returnValue: (int[] type) The size, where [0] is the width and [1] is the height.

function29.name: setComponentColor
function29.declaration: void setComponentColor( int componentID, int fr, int fg, int fb, int fa, int br, int bg, int bb, int ba )
function29.description: Sets the colors of a GUI component.
function29.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) fr : The red component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fg : The green component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fb : The blue component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) fa : The alpha component of the foreground color (0 to 255). 
(int type) br : The red component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) bg : The green component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) bb : The blue component of the background color (0 to 255). 
(int type) ba : The alpha component of the background color (0 to 255).

function30.name: setComponentColor
function30.declaration: void setComponentColor( int componentID, int fgRGBA[ ], int bgRGBA[ ] )
function30.description: Sets the colors of a GUI component.
function30.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int[] type) fgRGBA : The array storing the foreground color components, where [0] is red, [1] is green, [2] is blue, and [3] is alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255. 
(int[] type) bgRGBA : The array storing the background color components, where [0] is red, [1] is green, [2] is blue, and [3] is alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function31.name: getComponentColor
function31.description: Returns the colors of a GUI component.
function31.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
function31.returnValue: An int[][] array storing the color components, where [0][0] is the foreground red, [0][1] is the foreground green, [0][2] is the foreground blue, [0][3] is the foreground alpha, [1][0] is the background red, [1][1] is the background green, [1][2] is the background blue, and [1][3] is the background alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function54.name: setComponentIntArray
function54.declaration: void setComponentIntArray( int componentID, int value[ ] )
function54.description: Sets the int array value held by a GUI component.
function54.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int[] type) value : The value to hold.

function55.name: getComponentIntArray
function55.description: Returns the int array value held by a GUI component.
function55.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.

function56.name: addComponent
function56.declaration: void addComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function56.description: Mounts a GUI component on another GUI component. Since VCSSL 3, the newer mountComponent function has been supported. This function is supported for compatibility.
function56.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to mount. 
(int type) componentID : The ID of the destination GUI component.

function57.name: removeComponent
function57.declaration: void removeComponent( int componentID, int parentComponentID )
function57.description: Demounts a GUI component. Since VCSSL 3, the newer demountComponent function has been supported. This function is supported for compatibility.
function57.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the GUI component to demount. 
(int type) componentID : The ID of the current parent GUI component.

function58.name: newGraphicsLabel
function58.declaration: int newGraphicsLabel( int x, int y, int w, int h, int graphicsID )
function58.description: Creates a graphics label, which is a component displaying drawn content or an image, and returns its unique identifier (GUI component ID).Since VCSSL 3.3.24, the newer newImageLabel function has been supported.This function is supported for compatibility.
function58.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
width : The width. 
height : The height. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID of the displayed content.

function59.name: setComponentGraphics
function59.declaration: void setComponentGraphics( int componentID, int graphicsID )
function59.description: Sets the graphics resource ID of a GUI component.Since VCSSL 3.3.24, the newer setComponentImage function has been supported.This function is supported for compatibility.
function59.parameters:
(int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.

function60.name: getComponentGraphics
function60.description: Returns the graphics resource ID of a GUI component.Since VCSSL 3.3.24, the newer getComponentImage function has been supported.This function is supported for compatibility.
function60.parameters: (int type) componentID : The ID of the target GUI component.
